channel.title: Requirements Engineering on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Requirements Engineering on CEDIS

item.title: Requirements Engineering
item.description: Requirements engineering (RE) is an interdisciplinary area of Software Engineering aimed at bridging the gap between the acquirer’s and the supplier’s or developer’s domains.

item.title: People
item.description: Associate Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the Software Engineering undergraduate program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing with a focus on requirements, human aspects, and software engineering education.

item.title: Products
item.description: 2025 technological product proposing a chatbot as a support tool for Requirements Engineering.

item.title: Counting function points without humans counting: a dissertation reads HTTP requests to automate SFP
item.description: Diego Emanuel Ferreira da Rocha presented at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program the qualification exam of a dissertation that automates elementary-process identification through HTTP request analysis — reducing the friction of Simple Function Points.

item.title: Chat gpt 4o in requirements elicitation an undergraduate thesis runs the case study the community needed
item.description: Geraldo Victor Alves Barbosa and João Victor de Oliveira Matos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating the use of ChatGPT-4o in the requirements elicitation, discovery, and specification process.

item.title: Improving the requirements process: an undergraduate thesis brings rigor to development's most volatile cycle
item.description: Giulia Lobo Barros defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis investigating improvement of the software requirements engineering process — under joint advising of Cristiane Ramos and Ricardo Ajax.

item.title: FormsGeo: a thesis adds geolocation to social surveys and returns context to respondents
item.description: Marcelo Victor de Sousa Silva and Washington Bispo Arishita Júnior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis introducing FormsGeo — an app for social surveys with built-in geolocation.

item.title: Serious games for ADHD: an undergraduate thesis surfaces requirements the literature usually skips
item.description: Gabriel Batista Albino Silva and Helena Bretas Goulart defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis identifying non-functional requirements for serious games aimed at children with Attention-Deficit / Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD).

item.title: REQGAME: a dissertation tests gamification for requirements elicitation in a federal public agency
item.description: Ana Cláudia Alves Mendes Araújo defended at UnB’s PPCA a dissertation applying gamification to the requirements elicitation process in software projects run at a federal public agency.

item.title: Reading what's already logged: an undergraduate thesis proposes planning process improvements from issues
item.description: Sannya Santana de Arvelos defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis proposing the use of issue analysis as the basis for identifying software process improvement opportunities.

item.title: Measure to engage: an undergraduate thesis proposes a strategy to improve gamified applications
item.description: Paulo Markes Rodrigues de Oliveira Calado defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis developing a strategy to identify improvement opportunities in gamified applications — tackling the problem that the initial shine does not always translate into prolonged use.

item.title: Choosing a CMS without falling back on 'that's what we already use'
item.description: Thiago Silveira Honorato defended an undergraduate thesis proposing a rational method to choose between Content Management Systems for different scenarios — reducing reliance on decisions based on team familiarity alone.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Dissertation by Diego Emanuel Ferreira da Rocha (2025).

item.title: Collaborative Digital Transformation Methodology for SPU
item.description: Ongoing project based on service design and user experience for digital transformation at SPU.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Geraldo Victor Alves Barbosa, João Victor de Oliveira Matos (2025).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Yuri Alves Bacarias (2025).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Brenno Oliveira Silva, Paulo Vitor Silva Abi Acl (2025).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Workshop by ERVEN, RICARDO CORDEIRO GALVÃO SANTANA VAN, DIAS, POLLYANNA C. O., JUBÉ, DEMÉTRIUS DE ALMEIDA, CORRÊA, GEORGE MARSICANO, CANEDO, EDNA DIAS (2023).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Marcelo Victor de Sousa Silva, Washington Bispo Arishita Júnior (2023).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Giulia Lobo Barros (2023).

item.title: Improving Digital Public Service for Citizens
item.description: Closed project focused on improving digital public services for citizens.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Gabriel Batista Albino Silva, Helena Bretas Goulart (2022).

item.title: Publications
item.description: The success of a software project is closely linked to the clarity and accuracy of its requirements. These requirements outline the project scope and establish the expected operation of the software. Thus, the requirement elicitation phase is a critical milestone in the software development lifecycle, demanding excellent communication, cooperation, and motivation skills among all stakeholders. However, challenges such as effective collaboration and scheduling coordination can compromise engagement and active participation in this process. To overcome these obstacles and stimulate participation, gamification has proven to be an effective strategy by applying game mechanics in non-game contexts. Within this framework, Octalysis, a gamification framework developed by Yu-Kai Chou, offers a methodology to tailor and customize gamification according to the user profiles involved. This study introduces REQGame, an innovative method that integrates gamification into the requirement elicitation process, based on the Octalysis framework. The aim is to enhance the involvement and engagement of participants, also evaluating the positive effects that the implementation of gamification techniques can bring to the process.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Sannya Santana de Arvelos (2020).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Tcc by Ronyell Henrique dos Santos (2020).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Article by Hugo Ferreira Martins, Antônio Carvalho de Oliveira Junior, Edna Dias Canedo, Ricardo Ajax Dias Kosloski, Roberto Ávila Paldês, Edgard Costa Oliveira (2019).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Conference by Angélica Toffano Seidel Calazans, Roberto Ávila Paldês, Edna Dias Canedo, Eloisa Toffano Seidel Masson, Fernando de Albuquerque Guimarães, Kiane Mabel Fialho Rezende, Emeli Braosi, Ricardo Ajax Dias Kosloski (2019).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Book by MARSICANO, G. C. (2018).

item.title: Publications
item.description: Currently, individuals are spending an increasing amount of time using devices connected to the internet, making it a challenge to capture and maintain the attention of a specific audience. Distraction occurs in an instant; a moment of inattention is all it takes for everyone to start checking notifications on their phones. The speed at which new technologies and software are adopted is only matched by the speed at which they are abandoned. Against this backdrop, an emerging strategy in various fields is the incorporation of playful elements as a means of fostering engagement, a concept known as gamification. While gamification proves to be a promising approach, it is essential that it continuously evolves to remain appealing and aligned with the expectations and development of its audience. A thorough analysis of a gamified initiative, aimed at identifying areas for improvement, is crucial for determining directions for evolution. Therefore, this study aims to define a strategy that enables the identification of potential improvements in gamified applications. Such improvements are highlighted by the evaluation of project indicators that measure usability and by direct feedback from users after testing the application. Direct interaction with users allows adjustments to be informed by the perspectives of those the project is intended for. The convergence of users’ views in favor of the suggested improvements validates the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Conference by MARSICANO, GEORGE, MENDES, FABIANA FREITAS, FERNANDES, MAURICIO VIDOTTI, FREITAS, S. A. A. (2016).
